title: dryopteris filix-mas var. crinita
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas var. crinita, also known as the hairy male fern, is a variety of the common male fern, a species of flowering plant in the fern family (Dryopteridaceae). The hairy male fern has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it beneficial for treating various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions. Its medicinal properties can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and enhancing antioxidant activity. The hairy male fern can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
